title: Healthcare infection prevention and control guidelines - Canada.ca
content:
## Healthcare infection prevention and control guidelines
Guidelines to help prevent the spread of infection in healthcare settings. These guidelines were developed by the Public Health Agency of Canada with the support of the National Advisory Committee on Infection Prevention and Control (NAC-IPC) .
- About healthcare-associated infections
- Healthcare infection prevention and control guidelines

## About healthcare-associated infections
Healthcare-associated infections (HAIs) are infections that can spread during the provision of health care. HAIs are also known as nosocomial infections or hospital-acquired infections.
Implementing infection prevention and control guidelines can help prevent the spread of HAIs.
HAIs can spread from:
- person to person (patients, healthcare workers and visitors)
- environment (reusable patient care equipment, surfaces) to person

## Healthcare infection prevention and control guidelines
- Routine Practices and Additional Precautions for Preventing the Transmission of Infection in Healthcare Settings (2013) Disclaimer: The recommendations for hantavirus in Routine Practices and Additional Precautions for Preventing the Transmission of Infection in Healthcare Settings do not apply to hantavirus Andes species (ANDV).
- Disclaimer: The recommendations for hantavirus in Routine Practices and Additional Precautions for Preventing the Transmission of Infection in Healthcare Settings do not apply to hantavirus Andes species (ANDV).
- Hand Hygiene Practices in Healthcare Settings (2012)
- Prevention and Control of Occupational Infections in Health Care (2002)
- Notice: Updated infection prevention and control recommendations for Measles in healthcare settings
- Notice: Interim recommendations for infection prevention and control of avian influenza in healthcare settings

- Carbapenemase-Producing Enterobacterales (CPE)       Infection Prevention and Control in Canadian Healthcare Settings
- Guidance: Candida auris Infection Prevention and Control in Canadian Healthcare Settings (2024)
- Poster: Help reduce the spread of antimicrobial resistance - Follow recommendations for routine practices in settings where health care is provided (2016)

### Bloodborne viruses (HIV, hepatitis B and hepatitis C)
- Guideline on the Prevention of Transmission of Bloodborne Viruses from Infected Healthcare Workers in Healthcare Settings (2019)

- Classic Creutzfeldt-Jakob Disease in Canada, Quick Reference Guide (2007)

### Clostridioides difficile infection
- Clostridium Difficile Infection - Infection Prevention and Control Guidance for Management in Long-term Care Facilities (2013)
- Clostridium Difficile Infection - Infection Prevention and Control Guidance for Management in Acute Care Settings (2013)
- Infection prevention and control measures for Ebola disease in acute care settings (2023)

### Endoscopy-related infections
- Infection Prevention and Control Guideline for Flexible Gastrointestinal Endoscopy and Flexible Bronchoscopy (2011)

### Middle East respiratory syndrome coronavirus (MERS-CoV)
- Infection Prevention and Control Guidance for Middle East Respiratory Syndrome Coronavirus (MERS-CoV) in Acute Care Settings (2016)

- Canadian Tuberculosis Standards, 8th edition
- Critical Appraisal Tool Kit (2014)
- About: Canadian Nosocomial Infection Surveillance Program (CNISP)
- CNISP: Infection rates of national healthcare-acquired infections and antimicrobial-resistant organisms and antimicrobial usage in hospitals
- COVID-19 for health professionals: Infection prevention and control
